items.headline: Auto sales use proposed for El Camino Real commercial site
items.summary: The Planning Commission is considering a special development permit to allow auto sales at 975 W. El Camino Real. The proposal involves no building expansion or exterior changes, only site improvements like repaving, restriping, and landscaping.

items.headline: 195-unit transit-oriented housing complex permitted near Berryessa
items.summary: A $32 million multi-family development at 825 Berryessa Station Way receives building permit, adding nearly 200 apartments near the Berryessa BART station. The project aligns with San José's transit-oriented development goals to increase housing near public transportation.

items.headline: Water bill assistance pilot program launches for eligible customers
items.summary: San José is introducing a Financial Assistance Pilot Program to help water customers manage costs. The program provides targeted relief for residents struggling with water bill payments.

items.headline: City faces potential litigation over Cuesta Park water main incident
items.summary: Mountain View entered closed session on May 12 to confer with legal counsel regarding potential litigation arising from a water main incident at Cuesta Park. The specifics of the incident and its impact were not disclosed in the public agenda.

items.headline: 27-unit townhome project with 5 affordable units proposed
items.summary: The Planning Commission considered a residential development featuring 27 three-story townhomes, of which 5 units would be affordable. This project represents a significant infill development for Cupertino's housing stock.

items.headline: Active lawsuit: Fantozzi v. City and SummerHill Homes over real estate matter
items.summary: A litigation matter (Case No. 26CV492787) involving a plaintiff named Mark Fantozzi against the City of Cupertino and SummerHill Homes, LLC is ongoing. Details of the dispute were discussed in closed session.

items.headline: Council to hear 72-unit affordable housing project at Lytton Avenue
items.summary: A public hearing is scheduled for a six-story, 100% affordable housing development with 72 units on a 0.43-acre city-owned parking lot at 450 Lytton Avenue. The project requests state density bonus concessions and waivers, and the city is evaluating CEQA exemption eligibility.

items.headline: Campbell adopts policy restricting use of city property for federal civil enforcement
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution prohibiting city-owned and city-operated property from being used for federal civil enforcement activities. This policy limits how federal agencies can access or utilize municipal facilities and resources.
